items.title: Hermes Agent Website
items.text: This website showcases the Hermes Agent AI assistant - its capabilities, skills, activities, and documentation.

items.title: About Hermes Agent
items.text: Hermes Agent is an intelligent AI assistant created by Nous Research. It is helpful, knowledgeable, and direct, assisting users with a wide range of tasks including answering questions, writing and editing code, analyzing information, creative work, and executing actions via its tools.

items.title: Features
items.text:
• Multi-tool Integration: Access to web search, file operations, terminal commands, browser automation, and more
  • Skill System: Extensible skill framework for adding new capabilities
  • Automation: Cron jobs for scheduled tasks and background processes
  • Version Control: Git integration for skills and configurations
  • Computer Use: Background desktop automation capabilities
  • Delegation: Spawn subagents for parallel task execution

items.title: Skills Categories
items.text:
Hermes Agent skills are organized into categories:

  • autonomous-ai-agents: Spawning and orchestrating AI coding agents
  • computer-use: Background desktop automation
  • creative: ASCII art, diagrams, design tools
  • data-science: Jupyter notebooks and data analysis
  • email: Email management via terminal
  • github: GitHub workflow management
  • hermes-file-operations: Safe file operations
  • media: GIF search, YouTube transcripts, music generation
  • mlops: Machine learning operations tools
  • note-taking: Obsidian integration
  • productivity: Document creation, presentations, spreadsheets
  • research: Academic research, paper discovery
  • smart-home: Philips Hue light control
  • social-media: X/Twitter integration
  • software-development: Debugging, testing, code review
  • yuanbao: User grouping and mentions
  • zotero-library-organizer: Zotero reference management

items.title: Recent Activities
items.text: The Hermes Agent continuously learns and improves through: - Daily AI briefing system with Arxiv paper detection - Automated skill version control with hourly commits - Zotero library organization with automated Arxiv paper filing - Continuous integration and deployment workflows

items.title: Documentation
items.text: For detailed documentation, visit the Hermes Agent Documentation.

items.title: Getting Started
items.text: To use Hermes Agent: 1. Install the Hermes Agent software 2. Configure your API keys and preferences 3. Load skills as needed for your tasks 4. Start chatting and delegating tasks!

items.title: Contributing
items.text: We welcome contributions to the Hermes Agent ecosystem! See our CONTRIBUTING guidelines for more information.

items.title: License
items.text: This project is licensed under the MIT License.

items.title: Contributing to Hermes Agent Website
items.text: Thank you for considering contributing to the Hermes Agent website! This document outlines the process and guidelines for contributing.

items.title: 🤝 How to Contribute
items.text:
1. Fork the repository on GitHub
  2. Clone your fork locally: git clone https://github.com/your-username/hermes-agent-website.git
  3. Create a branch for your changes: git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name
  4. Make your changes to the website content in the docs/ directory
  5. Commit your changes: git commit -m "Add feature: your feature description"
  6. Push to your fork: git push origin feature/your-feature-name
  7. Open a Pull Request against the main branch of this repository

items.title: 📝 Content Guidelines
items.text:
• Keep content accurate, up-to-date, and relevant to Hermes Agent capabilities
  • Use clear, concise language suitable for both technical and non-technical audiences
  • Include code blocks and examples where appropriate
  • Link to official documentation when referencing specific features
  • Use markdown formatting consistently

items.title: 🐛 Reporting Issues
items.text: If you find issues with the website content: 1. Check if the issue has already been reported in the Issues section 2. If not, open a new issue with a clear title and description 3. Include steps to reproduce if applicable 4. Label appropriately (bug, documentation, enhancement, etc.)

items.title: 💡 Suggesting Features
items.text: To suggest new content or features for the website: 1. Open an issue with the label "enhancement" 2. Describe what you'd like to see added 3. Explain why it would be valuable to users 4. Provide any relevant examples or references

items.title: 📄 Documentation Standards
items.text:
• Use proper markdown syntax
  • Keep line lengths reasonable (under 100 characters)
  • Use descriptive headings and subheadings
  • Include alt text for images
  • Link to relevant sections within the document when appropriate

items.title: 🔧 Development Setup
items.text: For local development with Zensical: 1. Install Zensical: pip install zensical (or use the provided virtual environment) 2. Clone the repository 3. Run zensical serve to preview changes locally 4. Validate markdown syntax before submitting

items.title: 🙏 Thank You!
items.text: Your contributions help improve the Hermes Agent experience for everyone. Thank you for taking the time to contribute!

items.title: Headers
items.text:
# H1 Header
## H2 Header
### H3 Header
#### H4 Header
##### H5 Header
###### H6 Header

items.title: Text formatting
items.text:
**bold text**
*italic text*
***bold and italic***
~~strikethrough~~
`inline code`

items.title: Links and images
items.text:
[Link text](https://example.com)
[Link with title](https://example.com "Hover title")
![Alt text](image.jpg)
![Image with title](image.jpg "Image title")

items.title: Lists
items.text:
Unordered:

- Item 1
- Item 2
  - Nested item

Ordered:

1. First item
2. Second item
3. Third item

items.title: Blockquotes
items.text:
> This is a blockquote
> Multiple lines
>> Nested quote

items.title: Code blocks
items.text:
```javascript
function hello() {
  console.log("Hello, world!");
}
```

items.title: Tables
items.text:
| Header 1 | Header 2 | Header 3 |
|----------|----------|----------|
| Row 1    | Data     | Data     |
| Row 2    | Data     | Data     |

items.title: Horizontal rule
items.text:
---
or
***
or
___

items.title: Task lists
items.text:
- [x] Completed task
- [ ] Incomplete task
- [ ] Another task

items.title: Escaping characters
items.text: Use backslash to escape: \* \_ \# \`

items.title: Line breaks
items.text:
End a line with two spaces  
to create a line break.

Or use a blank line for a new paragraph.
